summary: Unmarried men face a substantially higher risk of accidental death than unmarried women, indicating a strong gender-based difference in mortality associated with marital status. Furthermore, unmarried individuals with cancer experience poorer prognoses, which also show potential gender-related disparities affecting treatment access and survival outcomes.
